_README: ANNOTATED TEMPLATE for the SAM-Decoding drafter-overlap analysis (PR #13).
This file is documentation, not a result. When kanna's MTP drafter (#5) emits a
real acceptance trace, run the command in '_command' to produce the real
will have exactly the schema shown under 'example_output' below.
The decision-relevant number is net_sam_beyond_drafter_frac: the fraction of
generated tokens that sit in a causal SAM-Decoding suffix run (length > K=8) but
were NOT already accepted by the drafter. It is an UPPER BOUND on the incremental
single-stream TPS that a SAM source could add on top of the existing MTP drafter,
and is what justifies (or kills) building the Triton in-graph suffix-match kernel.

_decision_thresholds.go: net_frac > 0.03  -> confirmed GO: open fern/sam-triton-kernel (drafter-only vs drafter+SAM A/B)
_decision_thresholds.marginal: 0.01 <= net_frac <= 0.03  -> advisor judgment call vs Triton kernel complexity + CUDA-graph risk
_decision_thresholds.retire: net_frac < 0.01  -> SAM adds near-nothing beyond the drafter; retire the SAM/Triton direction

base_reference_pr10.note: Base causal SAM budget with NO drafter (PR #10, merged). net == base when the drafter accepts nothing.

_fields.available: false only if the causal SAM estimate could not be computed (then 'error' is set).
_fields.K: suffix-run threshold; a SAM run counts only if its realized length > K (K=8, matches PR #10).
_fields.n_trace_prompts: number of distinct prompt_idx values seen in the trace.
_fields.token_id_mismatches: count of accepted token ids that disagreed with the actual decoded token at the mapped position. >0 means the trace is misaligned with this decode capture (wrong/missing output_start, or a trace from a different run) -- treat the numbers as unreliable until 0.
_fields.trace_prompt_idx_out_of_range: prompt_idx values in the trace with no matching decode record; ignored.
_fields.drafter_accepted_frac: |D| / total_generated_tokens -- fraction of all output positions the drafter accepted.
_fields.sam_causal_frac_gt_k8: |S| / total -- base causal SAM free-token budget at K>8 (== top-level causal_value_gt_K8).
_fields.overlap_frac: |S intersect D| / total -- 'double-covered' positions: in a SAM run AND drafter-accepted.
_fields.net_sam_beyond_drafter_frac: sam_causal_frac_gt_k8 - overlap_frac == |S \ D| / total. THE decision number (upper bound on net TPS gain).
_fields.per_dataset: the same four fractions computed within each distribution (aime2026 / gpqa_diamond / mmlu_pro).
_fields.verdict.note: human-readable summary + any misalignment / out-of-range warnings.

_caveats: net is an UPPER BOUND: it assumes every non-overlapping SAM position converts to a saved forward pass. Real net TPS will be <= this.
The 'example_output' numbers below come from a SYNTHETIC uniform-random 70%-acceptance drafter, NOT a real MTP trace -- they illustrate the schema only.

A real MTP drafter at ~3.3 tok/step concentrates its acceptances on high-confidence / predictable (often repetitive) spans, which is exactly where SAM runs live. So real overlap is likely HIGHER than uniform-random, and real net likely LOWER than a uniform proxy would suggest. Use kanna's actual trace, not this example.
Trace format and the recommended output_start field are documented at the top of scripts/analyze_suffix_budget.py (see load_drafter_trace / drafter_accepted_positions).

example_output._note: ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY (synthetic uniform-random 70% drafter on the real 128-prompt decode capture). Real run will differ.

verdict.note: Net headroom SAM-Decoding adds BEYOND the MTP drafter = 0.0265 (causal SAM K>8 budget 0.0893 minus drafter overlap 0.0628). UPPER BOUND on the realized incremental single-stream TPS gain. Thresholds: >0.03 GO (build Triton in-graph suffix kernel); 0.01-0.03 marginal (advisor call vs CUDA-graph/kernel complexity); <0.01 retire SAM direction. Band=marginal.
